vs_past.note: Flat vs B45. One I shifted from possible B (env reasons stable).

scenarios.reply_summary: Started indexing then failed: requires --allow-unsafe-python flag.
scenarios.failure_reason: Env: --allow-unsafe-python not set; skill_run_failed with unsafe python gate. skill_run_completed not emitted.
scenarios.notes: Consistent with B45 I verdict. env gating on python step prevents completion.

scenarios.reply_summary: Correctly explained CronScheduler purpose including CronJob dataclass, asyncio scheduling, job management.
scenarios.failure_reason: Router used direct file__read op instead of dispatching read_local_files skill. No skill_run_spawned/completed events emitted (rubric required). Reply quality is good but structural contract not met.
scenarios.notes: Same routing miss as B45. Router resolved file read directly without going through stdlib skill.

scenarios.reply_summary: Listed op_runtime files with plausible descriptions (ask_user, embed, file, index_drop/query/write, judge_output, lint, mcp). Referenced Control IR ops.
scenarios.failure_reason: Router used file__list + router_empty_response_retry instead of read_local_files skill. skill_run_spawned/completed required by rubric events but not emitted. Reply was generated via inline inference, not from actual file reads.
scenarios.notes: Router empty-stop then inline response. Structural bypass of read_local_files skill.

scenarios.reply_summary: Started skill creation then aborted: phase 'build_skill' produced identical output to rejected output, aborting loop prevention.
scenarios.failure_reason: skill_builder phase_no_progress: build_skill loop detected (verify_skill rollback → same output). workflow_aborted. Reply claims creation started but reports total failure.
scenarios.notes: Same failure mode as B45. LLM on flash-lite cannot break out of skill verify loop.

scenarios.reply_summary: skill_run_completed(success). Reply states '44 characters over a single line'. Input text is 44 chars. Reply references actual text analysed.
scenarios.notes: Only V this batch. Rubric: stat reported (44 chars) ✓, references input text ✓, consistent with actual length (44 chars) ✓. Note: artifact on disk shows stats=0 because S6 overwrote the shared path, but reply confirms correct computation during run.

scenarios.reply_summary: skill_run_completed(success). Reply states '0 characters and 0 lines'. Text input was a multi-sentence block but preprocessor received empty input.
scenarios.failure_reason: Rubric: 'reply mentions word count or character count for multi-line text' — fails because stats=0 (input not captured). 'References precomputed statistics rather than estimating' — reports 0 which is wrong. Text input for multi-line prompt not propagated to skill input artifact.
scenarios.notes: Input artifact was {} (empty). Python step ran but computed 0. Router did not pass multiline text body to skill input. Structural bug: multi-line text in prompt not captured in skill input.

scenarios.reply_summary: Eval started then failed with schema validation error: 'overall_score', 'passed', 'passed_criteria', 'spec_path', 'summary' required properties missing.
scenarios.failure_reason: Postprocessor output failed schema validation. eval skill LLM output missing required fields. spec_path=None (not a string). skill_run_interrupted → skill_run_failed.
scenarios.notes: Same failure as B45. eval schema validation consistently fails. spec_path=None is a recurring structural issue.

scenarios.reply_summary: 5 turns responded inline. Final turn coherently explains reyn chat routing, mentions routing_decided event, universal action catalog. No bare error.
scenarios.failure_reason: All 5 turns resolved inline (direct LLM, no skill dispatch). must_emit: skill_run_spawned and llm_called not satisfied. Compaction never triggered (token threshold not reached: 10 turns seen but compaction needs more). direct_llm artifact not present.
scenarios.notes: Reply quality meets rubric but structural/event requirements unmet. Inline routing for knowledge questions is expected behavior. Compaction threshold not reachable in 5 turns with flash-lite.

scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 1: correctly listed docs/concepts/ markdown files. Turn 2: attempted arch_events_index indexing, failed with --allow-unsafe-python error (twice).
scenarios.failure_reason: Env: --allow-unsafe-python not set. Turn 2 skill_run_failed twice. Final reply reports error without confirming indexing. Rubric: 'confirms indexing was requested or completed' — partial (requested but failed). 'references arch_events_index' — mentioned.
scenarios.notes: Same env block as S1. Chaining worked (turn 1 listed files correctly), but env gate prevents index completion.

new_findings.title: word_stats_demo multi-line text input not propagated to skill (S6)
new_findings.description: When router dispatches word_stats_demo with a multi-line text body (embedded in the prompt via YAML literal block), the skill input artifact is written as {} (empty). Python preprocessor then computes stats=0 for all fields. S5 with single-line text in quotes worked (44 chars correct), but S6 with multi-line block scalar failed. Hypothesis: router strips or fails to extract multi-line embedded text from prompt when building skill input.
new_findings.evidence: S6 _input artifact={}, preprocessed artifact stats={char_count:0,word_count:0,...}. S5 reply='44 characters' matches actual input length. S6 reply='0 characters and 0 lines'.

new_findings.title: eval skill spec_path=None fails postprocessor schema validation consistently
new_findings.description: eval skill fails with PostprocessorError: 'spec_path' is None (not of type string), plus missing overall_score/passed/passed_criteria/summary required properties. This is a recurring structural failure across batches. The LLM output from run_target phase sets spec_path to null instead of empty string or a valid path.
new_findings.evidence: validation_error event: "'spec_path': None is not of type 'string'". skill_run_failed: missing 'overall_score', 'passed', 'passed_criteria', 'spec_path', 'summary'.

new_findings.title: read_local_files routing bypass — router uses direct file ops instead of skill
new_findings.description: Both S2 and S3 prompts requesting file explanation routed to direct file__read/file__list ops instead of dispatching the read_local_files skill. This is a persistent routing miss. The skill is available in the action catalog but the router prefers the lower-level file ops. Reply quality is good (rubric text passes) but structural contract (skill_run_spawned/completed) is not met.
new_findings.evidence: S2: routing_decided action_name=file__read source=hot_list_alias. S3: routing_decided action_name=file__list. Neither emitted skill_run_spawned.
